title: AODS Module v3
description: Knowledge module with typed artifact payload enforcement, capsule metadata hooks, and structured governance support.

context.description: Module summary. Agent reads first to decide load/skip. Answers: what does this define, when to use it.

assumptions.description: Preconditions agent must accept as true when using this module.

sections.description: Flat array of knowledge sections. Semantic order. Agent may jump via refs. Optional when the module is artifact-first and semantics live in structured artifacts.

artifacts.description: Structured resources referenced by sections or carried as the primary authority in an artifact-first module. Each artifact has a typed schema.

topic.description: Compressed noun phrase. Agent uses for section-level routing.

content.description: Knowledge payload in AOP. See spec-aop module.

content_type.description: prose=compressed natural language. rules=constraint list. enum=value enumeration. table=pipe-delimited rows. logic=conditional/branching. example=input-output pairs. glossary=term definitions.

artifact_refs.description: Artifact IDs in this module referenced by this section.

citation_refs.description: External citation IDs declared in module.meta.external_citations that support this section.

term_refs.description: Structured references to canonical glossary terms used by this section. Machine refs must use canonical glossary keys, not aliases.

type.description: Artifact type. Determines schema of content field.

content.description: Typed payload. Structure depends on type field. See type-specific definitions.

usage.description: When/how agent should use this artifact.

decision_provenance.description: Optional provenance boundary for agent-consumable decision artifacts. It identifies source anchors, evidence posture, and the consumption gate for derived or evidence-backed decisions.

citation_refs.description: External citation IDs declared in module.meta.external_citations that support this artifact.

term_refs.description: Structured references to canonical glossary terms used by this artifact. Machine refs must use canonical glossary keys, not aliases.

state_machine_content.description: TYPE: state-machine. Lifecycle modeling, status tracking, entity state management.

invariants.description: Conditions that must hold while in this state.

parallel_regions.description: Orthogonal regions for concurrent state tracking.

process_flow_content.description: TYPE: process-flow. Multi-step workflows with branching, parallelism, loops, sub-processes.

type.description: action=execute. decision=branch. parallel=concurrent. loop=iterate. subprocess=delegate. wait=pause. emit=send event. receive=await event.

next.description: String=single next step. Object={condition: step_id} for branches. Null=terminal.

loop_body.description: For type=loop. Step IDs per iteration.

on_timeout.description: For type=wait. Step ID on timeout.

sequence_content.description: TYPE: sequence. Multi-actor temporal interaction. Message exchange protocols.

decision_tree_content.description: TYPE: decision-tree. Complex conditional logic, diagnostic flows, classification, policy evaluation.

default.description: Fallback node if no branch matches.

delegate_ref.description: For delegate nodes. artifact_id or module_id:artifact_id.

data_pipeline_content.description: TYPE: data-pipeline. Data transformation chains. Data lineage and transformation logic.

transform.description: e.g. 'filter status=active', 'map: extract [name, email]', 'aggregate: group by region, sum revenue'.

topology_content.description: TYPE: topology. System architecture, component relationships, network structure, dependency graphs.

children.description: Nested node IDs for hierarchical topology.

rule_set_content.description: TYPE: rule-set. Business rules, policies, constraints, SLAs, access control.

exception.description: Skip rule if this is true.

mapping_table_content.description: TYPE: mapping-table. Routing tables, permission matrices, enum translations, config lookup.

surface_inventory_content.description: TYPE: surface-inventory. Declares current, reserved, and future surfaces relative to the corpus root or a repo root selected at validation time.

base.description: Path resolution base. corpus resolves from the corpus root. repo resolves from validate --repo-root when supplied; otherwise from the corpus root.

placeholder_files.description: Optional placeholder basenames treated as non-real material for directory checks.

path.description: Relative path resolved from the selected base.

event_catalog_content.description: TYPE: event-catalog. Event-driven architecture: events, triggers, emitters, consumers, payloads.

error_strategy_content.description: TYPE: error-strategy. Exception handling, recovery logic, fallback chains, circuit breakers.

api_contract_content.description: TYPE: api-contract. Inter-component interface definitions: endpoints, schemas, errors, SLAs.

input.description: Input schema: inline or artifact ref.

temporal_pattern_content.description: TYPE: temporal-pattern. Time-based behaviors: schedules, cron, rate limits, deadlines, SLA timers.

expression.description: Cron expr, duration, timestamp, or rate.

note.description: Optional explanatory fact tied to this temporal pattern.

code_template_content.description: TYPE: code-template. Source template string or structured template object.

config_template_content.description: TYPE: config-template. Configuration template string or structured template object.

prompt_template_content.description: TYPE: prompt-template. Prompt string or structured prompt object.

regex_content.description: TYPE: regex. Pattern string or structured regex object.

enum_def_content.description: TYPE: enum-def. Enumeration values and descriptions.

role.description: guide=orient or route. sensor=verify, detect, block, or audit. hybrid=both.

method.description: computational=deterministic rule or exact algorithm. inferential=heuristic or model-mediated judgment. hybrid=both.

timing.description: Phases where this control is expected to act.

memory_role.description: authority=canonical semantic source. working=task-time routing or scratch guidance. evidence=proof-bearing or audit material.

sources.description: Stable source anchors such as module_id:sid, artifact_id, corpus-contained relative paths, or named external source IDs.

confidence.description: Confidence posture of the module's claims after synthesis and review.

review_chain.description: Ordered review, approval, or handoff anchors that strengthen provenance.

citation_id.description: Module-local external citation identifier used by citation_refs.

locator.description: Source locator such as URL, repository path, paper DOI, standard section, or withheld locator label.

version_or_date.description: Publication date, retrieval date, commit, version, standard edition, or policy effective date.

term_id.description: Canonical glossary key. Aliases and deprecated terms are not valid machine refs.

target_ref.description: Optional local section, artifact, field, or surface ref affected by this term reference.

consumer_surface.description: agent-consumable decisions can be used by agents without re-reading the full evidence only when the gate permits it.

basis.description: source-evidence points directly at evidence or authority. derived-summary is synthesized and must preserve the source boundary. mixed combines both.

source_refs.description: Section or artifact refs that authorize the decision.

evidence_refs.description: Section or artifact refs that provide proof anchors for the decision.

summary_ref.description: Section or artifact ref for the derived summary when basis is derived-summary or mixed.

evidence_status.description: Currentness posture of the cited evidence boundary.

consumption_gate.description: Whether the decision is stable-consumable, needs review, blocked, or experimental.

review_chain.description: Review anchors that justify consuming a derived decision.

citation_refs.description: External citation IDs that support this provenance boundary without replacing source_refs or evidence_refs.

tools.description: Execution tools or agent capabilities expected by this module.

resources.description: Environment resources or state surfaces this module expects to read or touch.

side_effects.description: Externally visible effects or durable state transitions. Use ['none'] for read-only behavior.

telemetry.description: Observable runtime signals emitted while execution is in progress.

checkpoints.description: Named milestone anchors that make long execution paths inspectable and resumable.

outputs.description: Named durable outputs expected from this module's governed execution path.

has_sensitive_payloads.description: True when the module or governed surface may expose sensitive payloads.

sensitive_classes.description: Declared classes of sensitive payloads. Empty or omitted only when has_sensitive_payloads=false.

redaction_posture.description: Minimum exposure posture for sensitive payloads.

exposure_policy.description: Short policy that states who may consume the unredacted surface and what must be removed before broader exposure.

profile.description: v0.7 minimum contract completeness profile for stable consumption.

required_fields.description: Profile-required fields that this module or surface promises to carry.

read_model.description: Read-model freshness and watermark profile for stable read-only surfaces.

term_refs.description: Structured canonical glossary term refs that apply to this stable contract metadata.

capability.description: Metadata-only capability support and fallback posture. This does not create negotiation runtime behavior.

capability_id.description: Stable capability identifier for provider or consumer metadata comparison.

unsupported_reason.description: Required when support_status=unsupported or partial; explains why the capability cannot be consumed as fully supported.

fallback_posture.description: Metadata-only fallback posture for consumers. This does not select or execute providers.

degraded_behavior.description: Expected behavior when the capability is consumed in degraded mode.

consumer_action.description: Recommended consumer posture for this capability metadata.

evidence_refs.description: Optional implementation evidence ids that support the capability posture.

snapshot_id.description: Stable identifier for the exported read-model snapshot.

exported_at.description: ISO-like export timestamp or deterministic build timestamp for the snapshot.

source_watermark.description: Source-side watermark, commit, event offset, data version, or equivalent freshness anchor.

staleness.description: Currentness posture of the snapshot relative to its source.

partial_reason.description: Required by convention when staleness=partial to explain the missing or lagging source segment.

source.description: Authority or data source behind the read model.

fields.description: Stable fields exposed by the read model.

identity.description: Key or identity semantics for rows, records, or generated entries.

absence_semantics.description: Meaning of missing, empty, or withheld data.

failure_semantics.description: How generation or read failures are represented.

partial_semantics.description: How consumers should interpret partial or lagging snapshots.

implementation_evidence_status.description: Review posture for a declared implementation evidence anchor.

implementation_evidence_kind.description: Kind of evidence that supports the design-to-implementation linkage claim.

implementation_evidence_freshness_policy.description: When this evidence anchor should be reconsidered for freshness.

locator.description: Implementation-repo-relative file path, CI check name, exported symbol, or review locator for this evidence.

command.description: Optional human-run command that can refresh or inspect this evidence; validators do not execute it.

expected_signal.description: Observable pass signal or review output expected from the evidence anchor.

reviewed_at.description: Date when this evidence anchor was last reviewed. Declarative only; validators do not execute refresh commands.

expires_at.description: Date after which this evidence anchor should be treated as expired until reviewed again.

refresh_cadence.description: Declared review cadence for maintaining the evidence anchor.

authority_surface.description: Section or artifact ref that owns the evidence claim.

implementation_acceptance_status.description: Review posture for a declared implementation acceptance criterion.

implementation_acceptance_check_type.description: How this acceptance criterion is expected to be verified.

implementation_acceptance_blocking.description: Gate posture if this criterion is not satisfied.

surface_ref.description: Module section or artifact ref whose contract requirement this criterion verifies.

requirement.description: Short human-readable requirement that links contract intent to implementation evidence.

evidence_refs.description: IDs from module.meta.implementation.evidence[] that support this criterion.

validator_rule.description: Optional validator rule name that verifies this criterion.

authority_surface.description: Section or artifact ref that owns this acceptance criterion.

repo_id.description: ID of the linked implementation repository declared in manifest.project_topology.implementation_repos[].id.

paths.description: Implementation-relative files or directories that carry the module's current implementation.

status.description: Implementation posture for the linked repo/path set.

pr_refs.description: Optional PR, issue, or change references that support the linkage claim.

authority_surface.description: Section or artifact ref inside this module that owns the linkage claim.

evidence.description: Evidence anchors that make current or planned implementation linkage reviewable without executing external commands.

acceptance_criteria.description: Contract-to-evidence criteria that state why an implementation linkage is acceptable.

breaking_policy.description: Compatibility posture for stable or adapter-facing consumers.

compatibility_window.description: Declared support window for deprecated or versioned consumers.

removal_guidance.description: Migration or replacement guidance required before removed state.

external_citations.description: Module-local registry of external sources. Internal AODS refs remain authoritative; these records preserve external evidence posture.
